page: Microsoft Azure HB176-96rs_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the HBv4 family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this virtual machine features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For storage-intensive operations, the instance includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. It is also RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, facilitating low-latency, high-throughput networking across clusters. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity position this server as a resource-dense option for memory-intensive and data-heavy workloads. The combination of dedicated compute cores, RDMA networking, and large local storage makes this instance suitable for high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale databases, memory-bound simulations, and complex data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure HB176-96rs_v4 is an x86_64 server instance from the HBv4 family, designed for high-performance computing and memory-intensive workloads. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Additionally, it is RDMA-capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput cluster communication. The absence of a GPU and the high memory-to-core ratio make it highly cost-efficient for non-graphical, memory-bound tasks. This server is suited for large-scale databases, scientific simulations, and complex data processing pipelines.
